strategies.sellSignal: No sell signal in this row (bullish variant) — see GMMA Death Cross for bearish mirror
strategies.holdCondition: Fast and slow GMMA are converging/flat within 0.1% of each other (chop zone)
strategies.exitLong: Fast GMMA crosses back below slow GMMA, or price closes below fast GMMA
strategies.exitShort: Not applicable (long-only signal)
strategies.takeProfit: Prior swing high, or 2x ATR(14) from entry, whichever the user prefers
strategies.stopLoss: Below the most recent swing low, or 1.5x ATR(14) below entry
strategies.confirmationIndicators: Volume increasing on cross, RSI(14) > 50, ADX(14) > 20
strategies.candlestickRequirements: None required; bullish close on cross candle preferred
strategies.marketStructure: Favors HH/HL structure already in progress
strategies.machineLogic: IF GMMA_fast[t] <= GMMA_slow[t] AND GMMA_fast[t+1] > GMMA_slow[t+1] THEN BUY
strategies.notificationMessage: {symbol}: GMMA 6 short/6 long EMA ribbon bullish crossover on {timeframe}
strategies.commonFailureConditions: Sideways/choppy markets producing whipsaw crosses; low-volume crosses
strategies.relatedStrategies: GMMA Death Cross, Price above GMMA
strategies.description: Classic trend-following long entry when the faster Guppy Multiple Moving Average crosses above the slower one, signaling a potential shift to bullish momentum.

GLOSSARY — ID: unique row identifier | Strategy Name: name of the strategy/alert | Category: broad classification (Trend, Momentum, Volume, Volatility, Pattern, Smart Money, Quantitative) | Sub Category: finer classification | Buy Signal: exact bullish trigger condition | Sell Signal: exact bearish trigger condition | Hold Condition: when NOT to enter | Exit Long/Exit Short: conditions to close an existing position | Take Profit/Stop Loss: suggested exit levels (not guarantees) | Confirmation Indicators: indicators that increase confidence but are not mandatory | Required Indicators: indicators mandatory for the signal | Optional Indicators: nice-to-have confirmations | Candlestick Requirements: required candle pattern, if any | Chart Pattern: required chart pattern, if any | Market Structure: structural context (BOS, CHoCH, HH/HL, etc.) | Smart Money Concepts: ICT/SMC elements involved (OB, FVG, liquidity sweep, etc.) | Risk Level: qualitative risk (Low/Medium/High) | Confidence Score: 0-100 heuristic based on how established/common the technique is in literature — NOT a backtested win rate | Estimated Success Probability: historical probability ONLY where reputable published research exists, otherwise marked Unknown/Variable | Expected Move: qualitative expected size of the move | Expected Direction: Bullish/Bearish/Neutral | Typical Time To Play Out: rough typical duration, not a prediction | Best Timeframe: chart timeframe(s) the strategy is best suited for | Best Market: asset classes best suited for the strategy | Trending Market/Sideways Market/High Volatility/Low Volatility: Yes/No fit flags for market regime | Computational Difficulty: Easy/Medium/Hard to implement programmatically | Beginner Friendly: Yes/No | Machine Logic: plain-English IF...THEN rule | JSON Rule: machine-readable JSON version of the rule for import into software | Notification Title/Notification Message: suggested alert text (supports {symbol} and {timeframe} placeholders) | Priority: alert urgency (Low/Medium/High/Critical) | False Signal Risk: qualitative likelihood of a false positive | Common Failure Conditions: known scenarios where the strategy tends to fail | Related Strategies: similar strategies in this dataset | Alternative Strategies: substitute approaches | Description: one-sentence tooltip-ready summary. IMPORTANT: This dataset is for educational/informational alerting purposes only. No row constitutes financial advice, and no strategy guarantees profit. All 'Confidence Score' and probability-related fields are heuristic or explicitly marked Unknown/Variable per the source research brief's data-quality rules — none are fabricated statistics. Users should independently backtest any strategy on their own data before live use.
